description_long: This helicopter features a compact, angular fuselage with a matte black finish, giving it a tactical and modern appearance. The main rotor consists of two long, straight, gray blades mounted centrally on the roof, while the tail section is equipped with a small, perpendicular tail rotor. The cockpit is defined by a series of dark, rectangular windows that wrap around the front and sides, providing visibility for the pilot and co-pilot. Sturdy black landing skids run parallel beneath the fuselage, supported by crossbars for stability during landing. The overall design suggests a utilitarian, possibly military or police use, suitable for rapid deployment in urban environments.
description: A compact, matte black helicopter with angular lines, gray main rotor blades, and dark cockpit windows. It features sturdy landing skids and a small tail rotor, giving it a tactical, modern look.
description_view_0: Front view shows the angular cockpit windows, central rotor hub, and the symmetrical landing skids extending outward.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the elongated fuselage, the full length of the main rotor blades, and the parallel landing skids beneath.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the tail rotor, the tapering tail section, and the symmetry of the landing skids.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the main rotor, cockpit windows, and the tail rotor's position.
attribute_rotor_blades: Two long, straight main rotor blades, gray in color, with a rectangular cross-section and no visible markings. The blades appear to be made of composite material.
attribute_tail_rotor: A small, perpendicular tail rotor with two short blades, mounted at the end of the tail boom. The blades are dark in color and lack visible markings or protective structures.
attribute_landing_skids: Pair of parallel, black landing skids with a rectangular profile, supported by crossbars for stability. The skids appear to be made of metal or composite material.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Multiple rectangular cockpit windows wrap around the front and sides, dark-tinted with visible framing, providing wide visibility for the crew.
